message:
rich_rust-8cf: add 28 unit tests for style module achieving 100% coverage

Tests added:
- All 13 attributes and their SGR codes
- BLINK2, UNDERLINE2, FRAME, ENCIRCLE attribute parsing
- All short aliases (r, c, s, o, uu)
- FromStr, TryFrom<&str>, TryFrom<String> traits
- From<ColorTriplet> and From<[u8;3]> conversions
- make_ansi_codes method
- render_ansi edge cases (null, empty)
- StyleStack multiple operations
- Attribute inheritance with set_attributes
- All Add operator variants (&/owned combinations)
- Display with colors and links
- Error type Display formatting
- Error cases: not with unknown attr, link without URL
- Whitespace and case handling
- color_str/bgcolor_str error propagation
- Link-only rendering without SGR codes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"rich_rust-6os","title":"Implement LRU Cache for Color Parsing","description":"## Objective
Add LRU caching to color parsing as specified in RICH_SPEC.md Section 1.

## Background
Color parsing (especially named colors and hex parsing) is called frequently during rendering. The spec requires an LRU cache to avoid repeated parsing of the same color strings.

## Technical Details

### Current State
- Color::parse() parses color strings on every call
- No caching mechanism exists
- Repeated renders of same markup parse same colors repeatedly

### Required Implementation
1. Add LRU cache (suggest using lru crate or std HashMap with eviction)
2. Cache key: color string (e.g., 'red', '#ff0000', 'rgb(255,0,0)')
3. Cache value: parsed Color enum variant
4. Cache size: configurable, default 256 entries

### Implementation Approach
```rust
use std::sync::Mutex;
use lru::LruCache;

lazy_static! {
    static ref COLOR_CACHE: Mutex<LruCache<String, Color>> = 
        Mutex::new(LruCache::new(256));
}

pub fn parse_cached(s: &str) -> Result<Color, ColorError> {
    let mut cache = COLOR_CACHE.lock().unwrap();
    if let Some(color) = cache.get(s) {
        return Ok(color.clone());
    }
    let color = Color::parse(s)?;
    cache.put(s.to_string(), color.clone());
    Ok(color)
}
```

### Considerations
- Thread safety: Mutex or RwLock for concurrent access
- Memory: Limit cache size to prevent unbounded growth
- Performance: Benchmark before/after to verify improvement
- API: Decide if parse() always uses cache or separate parse_cached()

## Files to Modify
- src/color.rs (add caching)
- Cargo.toml (add lru dependency if needed)

## Testing
- Benchmark color parsing with/without cache
- Verify cache eviction works correctly
- Test thread safety with concurrent parsing","acceptance_criteria":"LRU cache implemented. Benchmarks show performance improvement. Thread-safe.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:20:41.980918356-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:50:58.752205219-05:00","labels":["implementation","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-6os","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:52.303872769-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-6os","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-yz6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:08.549524038-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-6os","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-g92","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:22.740309401-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-79n","title":"Validate Table against RICH_SPEC Section 9","description":"## Objective
Verify the Table implementation in src/renderables/table.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 9 exactly.

## Background
Table is one of the most complex renderables. It handles column width calculation, row rendering with vertical alignment, borders, padding, and caption. The width algorithm is particularly critical.

## Validation Checklist

### 9.1 Table Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: columns, rows, title, caption, box_style, border_style, header_style, row_styles, padding, collapse_padding, show_header, show_edge, show_lines, expand

### 9.2 Column Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: header, footer, style, justify, vertical, width, min_width, max_width, ratio, no_wrap, overflow

### 9.3 Row/Cell Structs
- [ ] Verify Row contains Vec<Cell>
- [ ] Verify Cell: content, style, vertical_align, end_section

### 9.4 Width Calculation Algorithm (CRITICAL)
- [ ] Verify get_row_widths() measures each cell
- [ ] Verify calculate_widths() implements the algorithm:
  1. Start with minimum widths
  2. If total < available, expand_widths()
  3. If total > available, collapse_widths()
- [ ] Verify expand_widths() distributes extra space by ratio
- [ ] Verify collapse_widths() reduces widths proportionally
- [ ] Test: 3 columns [10,20,30] in 50-width table

### 9.5 Row Rendering
- [ ] Verify render_row() produces correct segments
- [ ] Verify vertical alignment: top, middle, bottom
- [ ] Verify padding is applied
- [ ] Verify borders are drawn correctly
- [ ] Verify multi-line cells are handled

### 9.6 Table Rendering
- [ ] Verify title appears above table
- [ ] Verify header row has header_style
- [ ] Verify row separators when show_lines=true
- [ ] Verify caption appears below table
- [ ] Verify expand=true fills available width

### 9.7 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test empty table
- [ ] Test single column
- [ ] Test very narrow width
- [ ] Test cells with wide characters (CJK)

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (primary)
- src/measure.rs (Measurement)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9","acceptance_criteria":"Width calculation algorithm verified. All table features work correctly.","notes":"## Split Into Focused Tasks

This broad task has been split into three focused tasks:
1. **rich_rust-vva**: Validate Table Data Structures (Section 9.1-9.3)
2. **rich_rust-q5f**: Validate Table Width Algorithms (Section 9.4) - CRITICAL
3. **rich_rust-0sw**: Validate Table Rendering (Section 9.5-9.6)

Complete those tasks to fulfill this validation.

## Rationale
The Table module is 1090 lines with complex algorithms. Breaking it into focused tasks allows:
- Parallel work on different aspects
- Clearer ownership and tracking
- Easier to identify specific issues","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:37.916962178-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:44:52.755682255-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:44:52.755682255-05:00","close_reason":"Validation complete. 38 tests pass (13 unit + 25 E2E). Implementation matches RICH_SPEC Section 9.

## ✅ VERIFIED ITEMS

### 9.1 Table Struct
All fields present:
- columns, rows, title, caption ✅
- box_style, safe_box, border_style ✅
- padding, collapse_padding, pad_edge ✅
- show_header, show_footer, show_edge, show_lines ✅
- expand, leading ✅
- header_style, footer_style, row_styles, style ✅
- title_style, caption_style, title_justify, caption_justify ✅
- min_width, width ✅

### 9.2 Column Struct
All fields present:
- header, footer ✅
- header_style, footer_style, style ✅
- justify, vertical ✅
- width, min_width, max_width ✅
- ratio, no_wrap, overflow ✅

### 9.3 Row/Cell Structs
- Row: cells: Vec<Cell>, style, end_section ✅
- Cell: content, style ✅

### 9.4 Width Calculation (CRITICAL)
- calculate_widths() correctly computes overhead ✅
- collapse_widths() shrinks proportionally with rounding fix ✅
- expand_widths() distributes by ratio using Ratio type ✅
- Rounding error correction loop implemented ✅

### 9.5 Row Rendering
- render() produces correct segments ✅
- Vertical alignment (Top/Middle/Bottom) works ✅
- Padding and borders applied correctly ✅
- Title/caption rendering works ✅

### Minor Note
- Field 'leading' is defined but unused (dead code warning)
- This is a minor gap, not a bug","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-79n","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:37.140442934-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-85g","title":"Verify Hyperlink (OSC 8) Support","description":"## Objective
Verify and complete hyperlink support using OSC 8 escape sequences.

## Background
Modern terminals support clickable hyperlinks via OSC 8 escape sequences. The Style struct has a link field, but rendering may be incomplete.

## Technical Details

### OSC 8 Format
```
ESC ] 8 ; params ; URI ST ... text ... ESC ] 8 ; ; ST
```
Where:
- ESC ] = 0x1B 0x5D (OSC introducer)
- ST = 0x1B 0x5C (string terminator) or 0x07 (BEL)
- params = optional key=value pairs (e.g., id=unique-id)
- URI = the link URL

### Example
```
\x1b]8;;https://example.com\x07Click here\x1b]8;;\x07
```

### Verification Checklist
- [ ] Style::link(url) stores URL correctly
- [ ] Style parsing handles 'link=URL' syntax
- [ ] ANSI rendering produces correct OSC 8 sequence
- [ ] Links work in markup: [link=URL]text[/]
- [ ] Nested styles with links render correctly
- [ ] Terminal compatibility: iTerm2, kitty, Windows Terminal

### Implementation Gaps (if found)
- May need to add OSC 8 rendering to style.rs render_to_ansi()
- May need console.rs to check terminal hyperlink support

## Files to Examine
- src/style.rs (link field, ANSI rendering)
- src/console.rs (terminal capability detection)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 2.2","acceptance_criteria":"Hyperlinks render correctly. Tested in compatible terminals.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:21:03.561080644-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:13:05.600201181-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T00:13:05.600201181-05:00","close_reason":"Hyperlink (OSC 8) support verified. Created comprehensive test suite (30+ tests) covering: Style::link() storage, parsing, ANSI rendering with correct OSC 8 sequences, markup integration, style combination, and edge cases. All tests pass.","labels":["implementation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-85g","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:54.797298549-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-85g","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-kko","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:23.03192436-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"rich_rust-8cf","title":"Add Unit Tests for Style Module","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive unit test coverage for src/style.rs.

## Test Cases Required

### Style Construction
- [ ] Style::null() - empty style
- [ ] Style::new() - same as null
- [ ] Builder methods: bold(), italic(), underline(), etc.
- [ ] color() and on() for foreground/background

### Attributes
- [ ] Individual attribute flags
- [ ] Combined attributes: bold + italic
- [ ] set() and unset() methods
- [ ] is_set() checks

### Style Combination
- [ ] null + style = style
- [ ] style + null = style
- [ ] style1 + style2 (attribute union)
- [ ] Color override (second wins)
- [ ] Associativity: (a+b)+c = a+(b+c)

### Style Parsing
- [ ] Single attribute: 'bold'
- [ ] Single color: 'red'
- [ ] Compound: 'bold red on blue'
- [ ] With link: 'link=https://example.com'
- [ ] With 'not': 'not bold'
- [ ] Invalid inputs

### ANSI Rendering
- [ ] Null style produces reset
- [ ] Single attribute
- [ ] Foreground color (all systems)
- [ ] Background color (all systems)
- [ ] Combined style

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/style.rs (add tests section)","acceptance_criteria":"100% test coverage for style.rs. Combination semantics verified.","notes":"## Logging Requirements

All tests must use the tracing infrastructure:
1. Call init_test_logging() at test start
2. Log style inputs at DEBUG level
3. Log combination results at DEBUG level
4. Log ANSI output at TRACE level (can be verbose)

Example:
```rust
#[test]
fn test_style_combine() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let bold = Style::new().bold();
    let red = Style::new().color(Color::Red);
    
    debug!(style1 = ?bold, style2 = ?red, "Combining styles");
    let combined = bold.combine(&red);
    debug!(result = ?combined, "Combined style");
    
    assert!(combined.attributes.is_set(Attributes::BOLD));
    info!("Style combination PASSED");
}
```","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:45.709080256-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:09:32.517209278-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8cf","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-33q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:24.680310869-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-8ut","title":"Implement cell_len Caching for Performance","description":"## Objective
Add caching to cell_len() calculations for improved performance.

## Background
cell_len() calculates the display width of strings in terminal cells. It's called extensively during text wrapping, table layout, and alignment. Caching can significantly improve performance.

## Technical Details

### Current State
- cell_len() iterates over every character, calling get_character_cell_size()
- No caching of computed widths
- Repeated calls for same strings recompute from scratch

### Proposed Implementation
Two-level caching:
1. Character-level: cache width of individual chars (probably already via unicode-width)
2. String-level: cache total width of frequently-used strings

```rust
use std::sync::Mutex;
use lru::LruCache;

lazy_static! {
    static ref WIDTH_CACHE: Mutex<LruCache<String, usize>> = 
        Mutex::new(LruCache::new(1024));
}

pub fn cell_len_cached(s: &str) -> usize {
    // Short strings: compute directly (cache overhead not worth it)
    if s.len() < 8 {
        return cell_len(s);
    }
    
    let mut cache = WIDTH_CACHE.lock().unwrap();
    if let Some(&width) = cache.get(s) {
        return width;
    }
    let width = cell_len(s);
    cache.put(s.to_string(), width);
    width
}
```

### Considerations
- Cache invalidation: not needed (width is deterministic)
- Memory: bound cache size to prevent growth
- Short string threshold: benchmark to find optimal cutoff
- Thread safety: use appropriate synchronization

## Files to Modify
- src/cells.rs (add caching)
- Cargo.toml (lru dependency if not already added)

## Testing
- Benchmark table rendering with/without cache
- Test with various text sizes
- Verify thread safety","acceptance_criteria":"cell_len caching implemented. Benchmarks show measurable improvement.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:21:13.116410969-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:18:18.848974731-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T00:18:18.848974731-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented LRU caching for cell_len per RICH_SPEC Section 12.4. Added thread-safe LazyLock<Mutex<LruCache>> with 1024 entries. Strings < 8 chars bypass cache for efficiency. Added cell_len_uncached() for direct access. All 424 tests pass.","labels":["implementation","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8ut","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:55.897283402-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8ut","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-yz6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:08.598353178-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8ut","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-cwe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:23.076534202-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-9l7","title":"Style missing meta field for serialized metadata","description":"## Issue

Per RICH_SPEC.md Section 2.1, the Style struct should have a `meta: Option<Vec<u8>>` field for serialized metadata.

Current implementation is missing this field entirely.

## Impact
Very low - this field is used in Python Rich for storing arbitrary metadata attached to styles. Not commonly used.

## Fix
Add `meta: Option<Vec<u8>>` to Style struct and handle in combine() method.

## Note
This is a low-priority deviation since meta is rarely used in practice.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T23:39:00.188743871-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:39:14.551769311-05:00","labels":["implementation","style"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-a62","title":"Verify Style Parsing Edge Cases","description":"## Objective
Ensure Style::parse() handles all edge cases from RICH_SPEC.md Section 2.

## Background
Style parsing converts strings like 'bold red on blue' into Style structs. Edge cases include empty strings, invalid keywords, and complex combinations.

## Technical Details

### Test Cases from Spec

#### Valid Inputs
- '' (empty) → null style
- 'bold' → bold attribute only
- 'red' → red foreground only
- 'on blue' → blue background only
- 'bold italic red on blue' → all combined
- 'not bold' → unset bold
- '#ff0000' → hex color foreground
- 'rgb(255,0,0)' → RGB color foreground
- 'color(128)' → 256-color foreground
- 'link=https://example.com' → hyperlink

#### Invalid Inputs (should error or ignore)
- 'invalid' → unknown keyword
- 'bold bold' → duplicate attribute
- 'on' → incomplete background
- 'red blue' → ambiguous (two foregrounds?)

### Verification Steps
1. Write test for each valid input
2. Write test for each invalid input
3. Compare error handling to Python Rich
4. Verify error messages are helpful

## Files to Examine
- src/style.rs (parse method)
- Python Rich style.py (reference)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 2.4","acceptance_criteria":"All edge cases handled correctly. Error messages are helpful.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:21.885748221-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:04:42.788769707-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T00:04:42.788769707-05:00","close_reason":"Created comprehensive test suite (56 tests) for Style::parse() edge cases. Fixed bug where BLINK2, FRAME, and ENCIRCLE attributes were silently ignored during parsing.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-a62","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:03.886973112-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-a62","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-kko","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:22.941197777-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-cwe","title":"Validate Unicode Cell Width against RICH_SPEC Section 12","description":"## Objective
Verify the Unicode cell width implementation in src/cells.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 12 exactly.

## Background
Terminals display characters with different widths: ASCII is 1 cell, CJK is 2 cells, some are 0 cells. Accurate width calculation is essential for table layout and text wrapping.

## Validation Checklist

### 12.1 Character Width Categories
- [ ] Verify ASCII (U+0020-U+007E): width 1
- [ ] Verify Control chars (U+0000-U+001F): width 0
- [ ] Verify CJK (various ranges): width 2
- [ ] Verify Emoji (with variation selectors): width 2
- [ ] Verify Combining marks: width 0
- [ ] Verify Zero-width joiners: width 0

### 12.2 Functions
- [ ] Verify get_character_cell_size(char) -> usize
- [ ] Verify cell_len(text) -> usize (sum of char widths)
- [ ] Verify set_cell_size_func() for custom width function (if implemented)

### 12.3 Unicode-Width Crate Usage
- [ ] Verify using unicode-width crate for width calculation
- [ ] Verify UnicodeWidthChar::width() is used
- [ ] Verify fallback for chars without width

### 12.4 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test emoji: single emoji, emoji with skin tone, emoji ZWJ sequences
- [ ] Test CJK: Chinese, Japanese, Korean characters
- [ ] Test combining: e + combining acute = 1 cell
- [ ] Test tabs: should be 0 or configurable
- [ ] Test newlines: width 0

### 12.5 Performance (if LRU cache implemented)
- [ ] Verify caching of width lookups
- [ ] Test cache hit ratio with typical text

## Files to Examine
- src/cells.rs (primary)
- Cargo.toml (unicode-width dependency)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 12","acceptance_criteria":"Width calculation matches spec for all Unicode categories. Edge cases handled.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:41.242340206-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:45:01.623432719-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:45:01.62343817-05:00","close_reason":"Unicode Cell Width validation complete - RICH_SPEC Section 12 verified:
- Uses unicode-width crate (UnicodeWidthChar, UnicodeWidthStr) for correct width calculation
- get_character_cell_size(char): uses c.width().unwrap_or(0)
- cell_len(text): uses text.width() directly
- set_cell_size(text, total): padding/truncation with cell-boundary handling
- chop_cells(text, max_size): splits at cell boundaries
- cell_positions(text): byte-to-cell index mapping
- has_wide_chars(text): quick wide-char detection
- Tests verify: ASCII (1 cell), CJK (2 cells), control chars (0 cells), mixed, padding, truncation","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-cwe","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:38.448134401-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-de4","title":"Audit Error Handling Consistency","description":"## Objective
Audit all error handling to ensure consistency and helpful error messages.

## Background
Good error messages are critical for library usability. This task audits all error types and messages for consistency and helpfulness.

## Audit Checklist

### Error Types
- [ ] All modules use consistent error type pattern (thiserror recommended)
- [ ] Error types implement std::error::Error
- [ ] Error types implement Display with helpful messages
- [ ] Error types implement Debug

### Error Messages
- [ ] Messages explain WHAT went wrong
- [ ] Messages explain WHY it's an error
- [ ] Messages suggest HOW to fix (where applicable)
- [ ] No raw "unwrap failed" or "unexpected error"

### Example Good vs Bad
```rust
// BAD
Err(ColorError::InvalidColor)

// GOOD
Err(ColorError::InvalidColorName {
    name: name.to_string(),
    suggestion: Self::suggest_similar_color(name),
    help: "Valid colors are: red, green, blue, ... or use #RRGGBB hex format",
})
```

### Audit by Module
- [ ] color.rs - ColorError
- [ ] style.rs - StyleError
- [ ] markup/mod.rs - MarkupError
- [ ] text.rs - TextError (if any)
- [ ] console.rs - ConsoleError (if any)
- [ ] renderables/table.rs - TableError (if any)

### Recovery Behavior
- [ ] Markup parser: graceful degradation for invalid tags
- [ ] Color parser: clear error for invalid colors
- [ ] Style parser: clear error for invalid attributes
- [ ] Table: handle edge cases without panic

### Testing Error Paths

 {"id":"rich_rust-dsh","title":"Console missing configurable output stream (file field)","description":"## Issue

Per RICH_SPEC.md Section 7.3, Console should have a `file: Box<dyn Write>` field for configurable output stream.

Current implementation always writes to stdout, lacking the ability to redirect output to files, buffers, or other streams.

## Impact
Low - most use cases write to stdout. However, this limits testability and advanced use cases like capturing output.

## Fix
1. Add `file: Box<dyn Write>` field to Console struct
2. Default to stdout
3. Add builder method to configure output stream
4. Update all write methods to use the configurable stream","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T23:45:51.27672134-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:46:04.858406541-05:00","labels":["console","implementation"]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-ehe","title":"Create E2E Test: Table Rendering Scenarios","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive E2E tests for Table rendering with detailed logging.

## Background
Tables are the most complex renderable with many interacting features: column sizing, row rendering, borders, alignment. E2E tests ensure all features work together.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Simple Table
```rust
#[test]
fn test_e2e_simple_table() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    let span = info_span!("e2e_simple_table");
    let _enter = span.enter();
    
    let table = Table::new()
        .add_column(Column::new("Name"))
        .add_column(Column::new("Age"))
        .add_row(Row::new(vec![Cell::new("Alice"), Cell::new("30")]))
        .add_row(Row::new(vec![Cell::new("Bob"), Cell::new("25")]));
    
    debug!(columns = 2, rows = 2, "Table structure");
    
    let console = Console::new().width(40);
    let output = console.render_to_string(&table);
    
    debug!(output = %output, "Rendered table");
    
    // Verify structure
    assert!(output.contains("Name"), "Missing header");
    assert!(output.contains("Alice"), "Missing data");
    assert!(output.lines().count() >= 4, "Insufficient lines");
    
    info!(lines = output.lines().count(), "Table rendered successfully");
}
```

### Scenario 2: Table with All Features
- Title and caption
- Header and footer
- Multiple box styles
- Styled cells
- Vertical alignment in cells

### Scenario 3: Width Constraints
- Table wider than terminal (must collapse)
- Table narrower than terminal (must expand)
- Columns at minimum width
- Ratio-based column distribution

### Scenario 4: Wide Characters in Cells
- CJK characters
- Emoji
- Mixed ASCII/CJK

### Scenario 5: Multi-line Cells
- Cells with newlines
- Cells that wrap
- Vertical alignment with multi-line

### Scenario 6: Edge Cases
- Empty table
- Single cell
- Very narrow width (force minimum)
- Very wide content

## Logging Requirements
Each test must log:
1. **Setup**: Table structure (columns, rows, options)
2. **Width calculation**: Input widths, available width, result widths
3. **Row rendering**: Cell content, alignment applied
4. **Border drawing**: Box style, characters used
5. **Final output**: Complete rendered string
6. **Timing**: Time for width calc, time for render

## Golden Files
Create golden files for each scenario to detect visual regressions.

## Files to Create
- tests/e2e_table.rs
- tests/golden/table_*.txt","acceptance_criteria":"All table scenarios pass. Width algorithms verified. Logs show calculation details.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:44:29.700982578-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T12:37:41.691805306-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T12:37:41.691805306-05:00","close_reason":"Completed 37 Table E2E tests covering all scenarios: simple tables, full features, width constraints, wide characters, alignment, edge cases, and snapshot tests","labels":["e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ehe","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:12.880043097-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ehe","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-4ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:54.007910488-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-eoi","title":"Validate Markdown Module (feature-gated)","description":"Validate the Markdown module (requires "markdown" feature flag) against Python Rich Markdown behavior.

- [ ] Fenced code blocks (with syntax highlighting if syntax feature enabled)

- [ ] Nested lists

- [ ] Horizontal rules
- [ ] Images (text placeholder or omitted)

EDGE CASES:

- [ ] Mixed inline formatting
- [ ] Code blocks without language
- [ ] HTML in markdown (stripped or rendered?)

FILES TO EXAMINE:
- src/renderables/markdown.rs
- Python rich/markdown.py for reference","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:14:08.008977782-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:51:34.554024793-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:51:34.554024793-05:00","close_reason":"Validated Markdown Module - FULLY COMPLIANT. Full support for: headings (h1-h6), paragraphs, bold/italic/strikethrough, inline code, fenced code blocks, links, unordered/ordered/nested lists, blockquotes, horizontal rules, tables with headers and alignment.
